title: Studio Khara is now on Twitter
display_title: Studio Khara is now on Twitter
wikitext:
[]: Studio Khara is now on Twitter             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Mar 07, 2011 10:53 am Tadaa

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Mon Mar 07, 2011 11:49 am Advertising Gundam UC 3 and the new Macross Frontier movie for some reason.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Mon Mar 07, 2011 12:29 pm Hatsumi92 wrote: Advertising Gundam UC 3 and the new Macross Frontier movie for some reason.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Mar 07, 2011 12:34 pm Those who follow evageeks on twitter got notified the day of via a retweet of the announcement: http://twitter.com/#!/khara_inc/status/42539578510422016

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Mon Mar 07, 2011 12:41 pm Maybe we're getting closer to a release date for Q?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Mar 07, 2011 12:50 pm A.T. Fish wrote: Maybe we're getting closer to a release date for Q?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Mar 07, 2011 12:53 pm NAveryW wrote: We've been getting closer to a release date for Q since before the movies started production.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Mon Mar 07, 2011 12:54 pm ^True, but maybe that's a sign that a release date is approaching us, much like an Angel approaches Tokyo-3, slowly. "Oh my God, look, it's a release date, pattern orange!"

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Mar 07, 2011 1:02 pm A.T. Fish wrote: ^True, but maybe that's a sign that a release date is approaching us, much like an Angel approaches Tokyo-3, slowly. "Oh my God, look, it's a release date, pattern orange!"

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Mon Mar 07, 2011 3:58 pm drinian wrote: Evidently they've been doing digital production work on those. Interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Mar 07, 2011 7:21 pm Hatsumi92 wrote: Really? Wow they've been getting around lately. Didn't they do some storyboarding in Star Driver? I believe it was for the second opening.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Mon Mar 07, 2011 8:20 pm We should send them a link about A Song of Ice and Fire and warn them of the dangers of schedule slip. Or just spam their page, whatever.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Mar 07, 2011 9:00 pm Trajan wrote: We should send them a link about A Song of Ice and Fire and warn them of the dangers of schedule slip. Or just spam their page, whatever.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Mar 08, 2011 1:21 am Screw release dates. "It'll be done when it's done!" is the attitude more movies should take. The pressure to meet release is what hobbles many big budget films. Finish it, then tell us when to see it.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Tue Mar 08, 2011 8:22 pm Azathoth wrote: Well hell, at least A Dance with Dragons has a release date.

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Wed Mar 09, 2011 8:51 am Trajan wrote: Which will inevitably be pushed back.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Mar 09, 2011 11:43 am Hatsumi92 wrote: and the new Macross Frontier movie for some reason.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Wed Mar 09, 2011 12:46 pm Well, that's certainly a change from... like a month ago, when I searched for them and nothing came up. Instant follow.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Mar 09, 2011 1:00 pm gatotsu911 wrote: Well, that's certainly a change from... like a month ago, when I searched for them and nothing came up. Instant follow.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Wed Mar 09, 2011 3:06 pm ^I didn't know you knew Gatotsu beforehand...
